issue_title: Use social context to improve episode text
issue_body:
Add a visible context review step that turns the host and guest social links from setup into creator-approved names, brands, topics, and spelling hints, then applies those hints to captions, title moments, and callouts before export. This makes the product feel smarter and more accurate without exposing internal research or asking creators to manually fix every transcript detail.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
A creator can enter social links during episode setup, see a concise editable context summary for each speaker, approve or edit names/brands/topics/spelling hints, and observe those approved hints reflected in generated captions, title moments, visual callouts, and the exported episode summary. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes and includes coverage for the context-to-captions/callouts/export flow.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The core path from setup through presets, canvas, audio polish, moments, templates, and export already exists. The highest-value missing product behavior is making the social links materially improve the edit, which is central to the vision and directly improves the publishable result._
